title: Perfectly Packed
description: Optimize bundle space using the best of square packing technology!
body:
Bundles are a fantastic way to save up inventory space, by putting items of different kinds in a single slot! However, they are not as efficient as they could be, because they forgot about preserving something else: **graphical space**.

GUIs can get busy, and so it is important to save as much space as possible. To this end, we have employed the [very best in square packing technology](https://kingbird.myphotos.cc/packing/squares_in_squares.html) to make sure all of your item slots take as little space as possible.

![Examples of the new UI with packings of 11, 17, 29 and 51 slots, shown in a grid. The images in the grid are not packed properly, with noticeable gaps](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f317355f1ef7570f0f20c2662b4f2942b8f2d2af.png)

_Now updated to version 26.1.x with version 2.0.0!_ 

On top of the many code changes due to Minecraft's updates, this update introduces a configuration through [YACL](https://modrinth.com/mod/yacl), which can either be edited in a file, or through a GUI when [Mod Menu](https://modrinth.com/mod/modmenu) is installed (the latter is optional).

The configuration options give more fine-grained control over when and how Perfectly Packed uses its custom bundle layouts over the vanilla ones.

The release is still alpha due to missing features:
- Improvements to several square packings used in the mod have been [found and documented](https://kingbird.myphotos.cc/packing/squares_in_squares.html) since the last update and have yet to be implemented.
- Using custom stack sizes, it is possible for bundles to carry up to 99 items, which requires a bunch more square packings to be coded in.

![A bundle with the new UI filled with items in a non-regular packing. One of the slots is highlighted.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8dc50bee6cb90f9befe79bfcf06d0de24ce2fbf5_0.webp)

Enjoy a gameplay experience free of unbearable GUI clutter and marvel at the science of square packing!

Right now, the mod is available for Fabric 1.18.x, 1.19.4, 1.20.x, 1.21.1-3 and 26.1.x. I currently don't have plans to backport it to other 1.21.x versions.

P.S: people on Tumblr, I see you. Thanks for sharing the mod!
